edges.description: Disordered atrial electrical activity produces the arrhythmia phenotype clinically recognized as an irregular pulse.
Atrial fibrillation-related rhythm disturbance commonly manifests as palpitations.
intermediate_mechanisms: symptomatic perception of irregular atrial rhythm

edges.description: AF-related rhythm and hemodynamic disturbance can produce dyspnea.
intermediate_mechanisms: impaired atrial contribution to cardiac output

edges.description: AF symptom burden includes fatigue, often alongside dyspnea and intermittent symptoms.
Atrial thrombus formation creates risk for embolic stroke, which is the main target of anticoagulation.

nodes.description: Rapid atrial rates cause shortening of atrial refractory period and loss of rate adaptation, promoting AF maintenance. AF begets AF.

evidence.reference_title: Modulation of NOX2 causes obesity-mediated atrial fibrillation.
evidence.snippet: NOX2 inhibition normalized atrial action potential duration and abrogated obesity-mediated ion channel remodeling with reduced AF burden.

evidence.reference_title: Pathophysiology of Atrial Fibrillation and Approach to Therapy in Subjects Less than 60 Years Old.
evidence.snippet: Atrial fibrillation (AF) is an arrhythmia that affects the left atrium, cardiac function, and the patients' survival rate.

nodes.description: Loss of atrial contraction leads to blood stasis, particularly in the left atrial appendage, promoting thrombus formation and embolic stroke.

evidence.reference_title: Pathophysiology of Atrial Fibrillation and Approach to Therapy in Subjects Less than 60 Years Old.
evidence.snippet: Atrial fibrillation (AF) is an arrhythmia that affects the left atrium, cardiac function, and the patients' survival rate.

evidence.reference_title: Dyspnea in patients with atrial fibrillation: Mechanisms, assessment and an interdisciplinary and integrated care approach.
evidence.snippet: up to two-third of patients also complain of dyspnea as a predominant self-reported symptom.

evidence.reference_title: Fatigue, dyspnea, and intermittent symptoms are associated with treatment-seeking delay for symptoms of atrial fibrillation before diagnosis.
evidence.snippet: Experiencing fatigue, dyspnea and intermittent symptoms produced symptom representations and emotional and behavioral responses associated with treatment-seeking delay.

evidence.reference_title: KCNQ1 gain-of-function mutation in familial atrial fibrillation.
evidence.snippet: Atrial fibrillation (AF) is a common cardiac arrhythmia whose molecular etiology is poorly understood.

evidence.reference_title: Dyspnea in patients with atrial fibrillation: Mechanisms, assessment and an interdisciplinary and integrated care approach.
evidence.snippet: Palpitations are the cardinal symptom of AF and many AF therapies are targeted towards relieving this symptom.

evidence.reference_title: Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis of Direct Oral Anticoagulants Versus Warfarin in Atrial Fibrillation With Low Stroke Risk.
evidence.snippet: The primary outcome was the occurrence of stroke or systemic embolism.
